papers.title: Generating EUPHEMIA-compatible bids for flexible demand under imperfect information
papers.titleZh: 在不完全信息下生成符合EUPHEMIA的灵活需求投标
papers.abstract: Electricity procurement constitutes a significant share of operational costs for large electricity consumers, and thus exposure to extreme prices poses a substantial financial risk. This paper proposes a method to generate EUPHEMIA-compatible bids for flexible demand to enable their participation in the European day-ahead electricity market while minimizing risks. Two strategies are considered, resulting in two bid formats: hourly bids (HBs), representing flexibility via marginal price responsiveness through price-quantity pairs, and exclusive-group bids (EBs), representing flexibility via mutually exclusive operational schedules submitted at opportunity cost. Our method is evaluated on a hypothetical electrolyzer system and a real-world steel plant under different market conditions. Results show that the economic performance of each strategy depends on the operational characteristics of the load and market conditions. Under volatile market conditions, highly flexible systems achieve better economic outcomes with EBs, while less flexible systems with stronger intertemporal constraints perform better with HBs.

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 大型电力消费者面临电价波动带来的财务风险，需通过灵活用电降低采购成本。然而，在欧洲日前电力市场中，如何将具有跨时段运行约束的工业负荷灵活性转化为符合EUPHEMIA清算机制的投标，尚缺乏有效方法。现有研究多忽略实际约束或无法生成兼容的投标格式，导致难以实际应用。本文旨在填补这一空白，解决在价格不确定性下如何生成既可行又经济的投标策略。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 论文构建了一个两阶段随机混合整数规划模型，用于生成两类EUPHEMIA兼容投标：小时级投标（HBs）和互斥组投标（EBs）。模型考虑了电价预测不确定性，通过Lévy稳定分布残差与Cholesky分解生成相关价格场景，并采用滚动时域模拟实现动态决策。消费者作为价格接受者，其投标需满足自身运行约束，同时在不同价格情景下最小化带CVaR风险度量的期望成本。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 研究发现，不同灵活性系统的最优投标策略存在显著差异。高灵活性电解槽在价格剧烈波动时更适合使用EBs，因其可通过提交整套可行调度来规避部分成交风险；而受强跨时段约束的钢厂则更受益于HBs，能通过细粒度价格响应捕捉短期机会。这表明投标格式的选择应基于系统特性与市场环境，而非单一最优解。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 作者采用滚动时域框架结合K均值聚类进行场景缩减，提升计算可行性。利用CVaR控制尾部风险，使投标对极端价格更具鲁棒性。HBs通过分段报价表达边际响应，EBs则以互斥调度包体现机会成本。两种策略共享底层运行模型，但第一阶段决策变量与市场出清逻辑不同，从而揭示了灵活性表达方式对经济绩效的影响机制。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 该方法依赖于准确的价格与负荷预测，且假设消费者为价格接受者，未考虑策略性报价行为。此外，EBs的生成依赖预设调度集合，可能限制灵活性表达。尽管如此，论文提供了从实际工业系统到市场接口的完整建模路径，特别适合关注需求侧参与电力市场的研究者阅读，其方法框架可推广至其他具储能与生产约束的柔性负荷场景。

papers.title: Age of LLM: A Strategic 1v1 Benchmark for Reasoning, Diplomacy and Reliability of Large Language Models under Fog of War
papers.titleZh: Age of LLM：一个在战争迷雾下评估大语言模型推理、外交与可靠性的1v1战略基准
papers.abstract: We introduce Age of LLM, a turn-based 1v1 benchmark in which two LLMs face off on a 13x7 grid to destroy the enemy base. Three stressors are deliberate: fog of war, full diplomacy (messages, ceasefires, ultimatums; uranium kept secret), and a reliability dimension where every turn must follow a strict JSON schema and an illegal action is silently discarded. The engine is private and each match uses a fresh random map seed and opponent, mitigating the data contamination that affects public benchmarks. Models receive a (near) rule-only prompt with no build-order advice (two tactical seed phrases were present during data collection; see Section 2.7). We benchmark 15 reasoning models across 54 matches and 5,258 actions. Findings: (1) the nuclear rush dominates (78% on the rules-coherent v0.11+ sub-corpus; 85% corpus-wide) with a sole-launcher signature that is largely mechanical under secret-simultaneous launch rules, not a cognitive deterrence failure; (2) military conquest is rare but faster (12.3 vs 18.9 turns); (3) diplomacy is prolific yet almost never consummated; (4) ~58% of illegal actions are fog/state errors, making the illegal-action rate a measure of belief-tracking; (5) -- the least established, and the only one we label exploratory -- a weak link associates reliability with winning. The corpus is small, unbalanced and not side-swapped, so the ranking is a preliminary descriptive view, not a contribution. Beyond ranking, the turn-by-turn traces of actions and messages make the corpus a lens on how LLMs reason under adversarial uncertainty -- their belief-tracking, spontaneous deception, and per-model cognitive "personas" -- which we frame as a future research direction. We release the replay format, an isometric viewer and all replays; engine source on request.

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 为什么要研究这个问题？当前大多数大语言模型（LLM）的评测集中在单轮、全观测任务上，无法反映真实对抗决策中的不确定性与长期规划需求。本文指出，现有公开基准易受训练数据污染，且缺乏对信念追踪、隐蔽对手推理和结构化输出可靠性的测试。因此，作者提出一个新基准，专门考察LLM在信息不完全、动态变化和高风险交互环境下的综合能力。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 作者如何把问题抽象成模型？研究构建了一个基于13×7网格的回合制1v1战略游戏，两个LLM作为对手在‘战争迷雾’下竞争摧毁对方基地。模型需在资源管理、军事行动、核打击与外交谈判之间权衡，每回合输出符合严格JSON格式的至多三个动作及一条消息。胜负由核打击、坦克攻击、接受最后通牒或和平协议决定，非法动作被静默丢弃，以此测试模型在部分可观测环境下的持续推理与行为可靠性。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文真正发现或证明了什么？分析54场跨15个模型的比赛后发现：核突袭主导胜利路径（占78%），但并非因认知失败，而是由秘密同步发射机制导致的机械性结果；军事征服虽罕见却更快；外交提议频繁但极少成功；约58%的非法动作源于对战场状态的误判，表明非法动作率可作为信念追踪能力的指标；初步证据显示，输出可靠性比思考时间更能预测胜率。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 作者是如何得到这些结果的？通过自研私有引擎运行多轮LLM对战，确保地图随机生成、对手动态配对，防止数据泄露。每场比赛记录完整的动作序列与自由消息，形成可回放的轨迹数据集。采用规则驱动的错误分类方法识别非法动作原因，并结合统计相关性分析（如非法率与得分的关系）、行为模式挖掘（如欺骗性沟通）来揭示模型在策略、外交与可靠性方面的差异。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 这篇论文该如何评价？研究受限于样本量小、非平衡对阵和未交换角色，排名仅为初步描述。提示语中曾含战术引导短语，可能影响早期侦察与坦克生产的频率。尽管如此，该基准的价值在于提供了一个可细粒度分析LLM在对抗性不确定环境中推理、欺骗与稳定性行为的新视角，其回放数据集特别适合后续认知风格与信念维护机制的研究。

papers.title: World Models in Pieces: Structural Certification for General Agents
papers.abstract: In the big-world regime, agents cannot be universally capable and their ability is inevitably specialized across a world model in pieces. Consequently, standard uniform guarantees fail to distinguish between the understanding of critical bottlenecks and irrelevant failures. We first formalize this limitation by proving that general agents are not universal, rendering standard worst-case analysis uninformative. To overcome this, we introduce structural certification, a transition-local framework that maps bounded goal-conditioned performance to entry-wise guarantees on the agent's internal world model. Our main contribution is constructive. We provide algorithms that filter specific transitions using deep compositional goals and prove that a general agent on these goals has a structural world model with a $\mathcal{O}(1/n) + \mathcal{O}(δ)$ error bound. Conversely, this bound is tight in the small-$δ$ regime, whose existence is explicitly guaranteed by our certification. These results enable the certifiable deployment of general agents by localizing the specific transitions where long-horizon planning is reliable.

papers.title: Solving Inverse Problems of Chaotic Systems with Bidirectional Conditional Flow Matching
papers.abstract: Modeling chaotic systems is crucial yet challenging. Inverse problems in chaotic dynamics, namely inferring initial conditions from final states, remain largely unsolved because of ill-posedness, non-uniqueness, instability, and potentially chaotic time-reverse dynamics. We address this open problem with Bidirectional Conditional Flow Matching (Bi-CFM), which learns bidirectional mappings between distributions of initial and final states to capture the stochasticity of chaotic evolution and mitigate exponential error accumulation over time. Furthermore, for systems with conservation laws, we extend it to Conservation-constrained Bi-CFM (CBi-CFM). Across the classic Lorenz, Circuit, and high-dimensional Lorenz 96 systems, Bi-CFM improves five distribution-level metrics over baselines while achieving a speedup of more than two orders of magnitude. In the three-body planet-planet scattering problem in planetary dynamics, CBi-CFM better respects conservation laws, with conservation errors comparable to those of the ground truth. Finally, on real observations of globular clusters, collisional million-body systems shaped by $\sim 10^{10}$ years (10 Gyr) of evolution, our method represents an advance in accuracy, establishing a scalable route to solving inverse problems of long-timescale real-world chaotic dynamics.

papers.title: Grad Detect: Gradient-Based Hallucination Detection in LLMs
papers.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across diverse tasks, yet they remain prone to generating hallucinations. Detecting these hallucinations is critical for deploying LLMs reliably in high-stakes applications. We present Grad Detect, a gradient-based approach for predicting hallucinations by analyzing layer-wise gradient patterns from a single forward-backward pass during inference. Our method shows that the internal gradient structure of a model carries rich information about the correctness of its output. This information is not accessible through output-level signals alone. We evaluate Grad Detect on several Q&A benchmarks across both hallucination detection and model abstention prediction, where it consistently outperforms confidence-based and sampling-based baselines. Through comprehensive layer ablation studies across all eleven models from four architectural families, we find that the final five layers concentrate over 97% of the discriminative gradient signal, enabling efficient deployment with minimal performance loss. Grad Detect provides a unified framework for predicting multiple dimensions of LLM reliability, offering strong predictive performance alongside interpretable insights into where and how model failures originate.

papers.title: Can Scale Save Us From Plasticity Loss in Large Language Models?
papers.titleZh: 规模能否拯救大语言模型免于可塑性丧失？
papers.abstract: The loss of plasticity - the ability of a network to learn new information after having already learned older information - is a fundamental challenge in creating artificial neural networks capable of continual learning. Although this phenomenon has been known for decades, it has mostly been studied in older, relatively small architectures and rarely in natural-language domains. To determine whether loss of plasticity remains a problem in the modern transformer-based LLM paradigm, we study plasticity loss in GPT-style Transformer models trained on a multilingual continual learning problem. Consistent with prior work, we find evidence of plasticity loss across models ranging from 5M to 314M non-embedding parameters, as measured by deterioration on a held-out Vietnamese probing task. We further find that the onset of plasticity loss follows a predictable scaling law, growing sublinearly with model size. These results suggest that larger models may delay the measurable effects of plasticity loss, but that increasing parameter count alone is likely to be insufficient to completely prevent it. We also find evidence of plasticity loss under stationary multilingual training, challenging the view that the phenomenon is exclusive to continual learning with abrupt task changes. Overall, our results suggest that even large Transformer language models trained on natural-language will eventually lose the ability to efficiently adapt to new data after sufficiently long training, in both continual and stationary settings.

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 大语言模型在长期训练中可能逐渐失去学习新知识的能力，这种现象称为可塑性丧失，不同于灾难性遗忘，它关注的是模型适应能力的退化而非旧知识遗忘。尽管在小模型中有研究，但在真实自然语言环境下大规模模型是否也会出现此问题尚不清楚。本文旨在探究现代Transformer架构在多语言持续学习中是否存在可塑性丧失，并检验扩大模型规模是否能缓解这一根本性挑战。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 研究采用GPT风格的预归一化解码器Transformer，在八种语言上循环进行持续预训练，并定期插入越南语探测任务来评估模型学习新语言的能力。通过测量模型在未见过的越南语上的适应效率变化，量化其可塑性水平。实验涵盖从500万到3.14亿非嵌入参数的多种模型规模，确保架构一致性以隔离规模影响，从而构建一个可控的自然语言持续学习基准。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文首次在真实自然语言流中证实了大规模语言模型仍会经历可塑性丧失，且该现象随模型增大呈亚线性延迟，遵循幂律缩放规律。这意味着单纯增加参数虽能推迟问题出现，但无法彻底解决。此外，在平稳多语言混合训练中也观察到类似退化，表明任务突变并非必要条件。这些发现揭示了当前训练范式下模型适应能力的根本限制。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 作者通过固定架构比例和注意力头维度，系统性地比较不同规模模型的行为，排除结构差异干扰。使用验证损失曲线下面积作为学习效率指标，结合高频探测与移动平均平滑噪声，精确估计可塑性丧失起点。同时监测参数增长、休眠单元、注意力熵等内部特征，探索潜在关联机制，为诊断提供多维线索而非单一归因。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 研究虽识别出若干网络级相关现象，但未能建立可塑性丧失的确切因果机制，各指标与性能退化间关系不稳定。结论基于特定多语言设定，外推至其他任务需谨慎。然而，其严谨的实验设计、清晰的缩放规律揭示及对平稳训练中该现象的确认，使其成为理解大型模型长期训练动态的重要参考，最值得阅读的是其实验框架与缩放分析部分。

papers.title: InSight: Self-Guided Skill Acquisition via Steerable VLAs
papers.titleZh: InSight: 通过可引导的视觉-语言-动作模型实现自我指导的技能获取
papers.abstract: Vision-language-action (VLA) models can learn manipulation skills from demonstrations, but their capabilities are bounded by the skills in the training data. We present InSight, a framework that unlocks autonomous skill acquisition by rendering VLAs steerable at the primitive-action level (e.g., "move gripper to the bowl", "lift upward", "pour the bottle"). InSight consists of two primary stages: (1) an automated segmentation pipeline that partitions demonstrations into labeled primitives via VLM plan decomposition and end-effector poses to enable VLA primitive steerability, and (2) a VLM-guided data flywheel that identifies missing primitives required to accomplish a novel task, autonomously attempts demonstrations of the missing primitives with VLM-proposed low-level control, and automatically labels, stores, and integrates successful demonstrations into the VLA training set. We evaluate InSight across simulation and real-world manipulation tasks, including block flipping, drawer closing, sweeping, twisting, and pouring, without any human demonstrations of these target skills. Once learned, these primitives can be composed to execute novel, long-horizon tasks without additional human demonstrations. Our findings demonstrate that primitive steerability provides a practical foundation for continual skill acquisition in VLA policies. Project website: https://insight-vla.github.io.

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 传统视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型依赖人类演示来学习操作技能，但其能力受限于训练数据中的技能范围。当面对新任务（如倒水）时，若缺乏相关演示，模型无法执行。现有方法虽能利用大语言模型（VLM）进行高层规划，但不更新底层策略，导致技能无法积累。本文旨在解决机器人如何在无额外人工干预下自主发现并掌握缺失的基本动作。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 系统基于一个预训练的VLA模型，输入为双视角图像、末端执行器姿态和语言指令，输出为动作增量和进度信号。训练数据由人类远程操作演示自动分割为带标签的动作原语。在测试阶段，VLM将新任务分解为动作序列，识别出当前VLA不具备的‘原语缺口’，并通过低层控制器执行这些动作，成功片段被加入训练集并重新训练VLA，从而扩展其能力。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文提出了INSIGHT框架，首次实现了VLA模型在动作原语层面的可引导性和自主技能扩展。实验在仿真和真实机械臂上验证了无需目标技能的人类演示即可学会翻转积木、关闭抽屉、扭转瓶盖、倾倒物体及清扫等任务，其中倾倒任务成功率高达96%，复杂14步组合任务达80%。结果表明，原语级可引导性是实现持续技能学习的有效路径。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 核心在于两个阶段：一是通过VLM计划分解与夹爪状态、运动轨迹对齐，自动将演示分割为带语言标签的动作原语，使VLA具备原语级可控性；二是构建VLM引导的数据飞轮，由VLM识别原语缺口、提议运动参数、执行并验证结果，成功者回流训练。这种方法将VLM从测试时规划器转变为训练数据生成器，实现了闭环的自主技能获取。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 方法目前仅支持单轴运动的原语获取，限制了复杂动作的学习；依赖VLM完成检查处理分布外状态，可能不够鲁棒；且每次尝试需人工重置环境。尽管如此，该工作展示了将VLM深度整合进训练循环的价值。最值得阅读的是其系统设计与自动原语分割机制，为构建终身学习机器人提供了可行蓝图。
